mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail demand raises near-term revenue expectations for listed firms; higher 3-month momentum in RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the slope of 3-month RSAFS growth on 20-day S&P 500 returns is nonpositive over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows or Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals weakening household income and recession risk; when UNRATE crosses more than 0.3 percentage points above its 3-month prior level, S&P 500 20-day returns turn negative.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or have mean returns not below unconditional mean returns at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Large Federal Reserve rate cuts following tight conditions reduce discount rates and ease refinancing constraints; when FEDFUNDS falls by more than 50 basis points over 3 months, 60-day equity returns are positive.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day S&P 500 returns after triggered easing are not higher than unconditional 60-day returns over a 5-year rolling window or the estimated coefficient is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Housing weakness damages credit creation and household balance sheets only when labor income also weakens; simultaneous declines in HOUST and PAYEMS predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% over 6 months and PAYEMS 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-PAYEMS trigger has no incremental negative effect after controlling for each single series, or if triggered 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A break in industrial production indicates falling factory utilization and weaker operating leverage; when INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average, S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days are negative.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside-crossing events do not produce below-median 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation pressure combined with rising policy rates compresses equity valuation multiples; when CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated and FEDFUNDS is rising, S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days are negative.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth > 3% and FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS change is not negative in rolling regressions or triggered 60-day returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion supports aggregate income and reduces near-term default risk; stronger 3-month PAYEMS momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher PAYEMS 3-month momentum is not associated with positive 5-day returns in walk-forward tests or if its Rank IC is below 0.02 for three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts signals improving household formation and construction demand; when HOUST crosses above its 6-month moving average after a prior decline, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its 6-month moving average after falling over the prior 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST upside-crossing recoveries have mean 60-day S&P 500 returns at or below the unconditional mean, or if positive-hit rate is below 50% in triggered cases.
